channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Aussies were decidedly not happy about the 3G shutdown, according to new report
item.description: Following the shutdown of 3G networks across Australia, telco complaints have increased significantly, according to TIO
item.dc:creator: zac.kelly@futurenet.com (Zachariah Kelly), TechRadar

item.title: Musk wields chainsaw onstage at CPAC, touting DOGE cuts
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk joined the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) stage Thursday wielding a chainsaw, underscoring his efforts to slash the federal budget and workforce through President Trump's so-called Department of Government Efficiency panel.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: AT&T debuts SplitPay, making it easier to split the bill on shared plans
item.description: The upside of friends, roommates, or family members sharing a multiline phone plan is everyone has a lower monthly bill. The downside, however, is one person is ultimately responsible for both paying that bill—and gathering what is owed to him or her from their friends each month.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: These new cheap ANC headphones from a reliable brand look like wildly good value – 90 hours of battery, Hi-Res Audio, spatial sound… all for $50
item.description: Audio brand 1More has now released a new entry in its budget ANC headphone line, and you’re getting a lot for the price.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Amazon MGM Studios acquires the license to thrill as its gains full creative control of the entire James Bond franchise in landmark deal
item.description: The entertainment industry has been left shaken and stirred by Amazon's acquisition of the 007 brand.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Did Siri break the law? Apple's latest privacy complaint in France doesn't bode well
item.description: A French NGO is accusing Apple of violation of privacy, unlawful processing of personal data, and deceptive commercial practices. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft's new 'breakthrough' generative AI model is designed to 'create consistent and diverse gameplay' and could be used to preserve classic games
item.description: Microsoft announces new generative AI model that can understand game physics and controller actions, and potentially preserve classic games.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft’s new AI hub highlights apps that support NPUs
item.description: Applications that support the specialized AI chips on Copilot+ PCs have been rare, but Microsoft is looking to change that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hulu Clearing Out Titles in March 2025 - Here's What's Leaving
item.description: Hulu is removing various movies and shows from its platform in March 2025, but don't worry, it's mostly lesser-known titles with poor Rotten Tomatoes scores. Find out what's leaving Hulu this month.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Why HR professionals struggle with big data
item.description: Over the next 15 years, more than 12 million people will retire, while technological progress will lead to major changes in occupations. According to the World Economic Forum, almost half of the skills required of employees today will change in the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft’s first step to scalable quantum computing
item.description: There’s a race going on. Not the one to build artificial general intelligence, whatever that may be, but to deliver the first at-scale quantum computer that’s able to solve a class of massive problems that would take far too long using even the most powerful supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI-generated images can be art. They just can’t be photos
item.description: As a kid of the 1970s, I was fascinated by a short-lived art movement known as photorealism. The painters who practiced it created works that weren’t merely realistic. They were borderline indistinguishable from photographs—an extraordinary feat to pull off with oil on canvas.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Aryaka adds AI-powered observability to SASE platform
item.description: SASE vendor Aryaka is looking to alleviate network alert fatigue with a little help from AI. Today Aryaka announced its Winter 2025 platform update, introducing its new AI Observe tool for advanced threat detection and prevention.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: More used Seagate HDDs disguised as new: Fraudsters make it tough to spot the difference
item.description: The controversy surrounding used Seagate hard drives has significantly widened, with fraudsters now targeting the company’s IronWolf and IronWolf Pro NAS drives alongside the previously affected Exos server-grade HDDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Humanoid robots bust dance moves alongside humans
item.description: Unitree H1 robots dance at gala, blending AI and tradition. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson examines the convergence of cutting-edge technology and traditional culture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Generative AI vs. the software developer
item.description: If you aren’t aware of the tectonic shift that is generative artificial intelligence, then I can only assume your phone has been on Airplane mode for the last year. You can’t swing a laptop bag these days without knocking over four startups doing something with AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 4 takeaways from the Trump, Elon Musk interview
item.description: President Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk sat down for a joint interview with Fox News’s Sean Hannity and defended the administration’s massive overhaul of the federal government while giving some insights into their friendship.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Airbnb sues New Orleans after it adopts sweeping regulations governing short-term rentals
item.description: Airbnb is suing the City of New Orleans for requiring the company and other short-term rental platforms ensure properties they market are in compliance with city regulations.
item.dc:creator: Jack Brook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on a beach in Australia's Tasmania state
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How to set up a home network like a pro
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you ensure a smooth online experience with a reliable, secure home network setup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, PIX 11
